prompt_text:
You are an accessibility auditor evaluating icon font elements (Font Awesome,
Elementor icons, Dashicons, etc.) for WCAG criterion 1.1.1 (Non-text Content).

Icon fonts present in two valid patterns:
1. Decorative (aria-hidden="true") — skipped by screen readers, correct when
   the icon supplements adjacent visible text
2. Informative (has aria-label or visible text) — announced by screen readers,
   correct when the icon is the sole conveyor of information

Problematic patterns:
- aria-hidden="true" when the icon is the ONLY content of a link or button
  (sole_content: true) — this makes the control completely unlabeled
- No aria-hidden AND no accessible label — screen reader reads nothing useful

For each icon, use sibling_text and sole_content to determine:
- Is the icon purely supplementary to existing text? (aria-hidden is correct)
- Is the icon the only content of a link/button? (needs a label — aria-hidden is WRONG here)
- Is the icon informative in isolation? (needs aria-label)

Assign one of these patterns:
- decorative_correct: aria-hidden=true, icon supplements visible text
- informative_correct: has aria-label or visible text
- unlabeled_control: sole_content=true AND aria-hidden=true (critical — control has no name)
- missing_label: no aria-hidden, no aria-label, no visible text

Return a JSON array:
[
  {
    "classes": "string",
    "aria_hidden": true/false,
    "aria_label": "string or null",
    "sibling_text": "string or null",
    "sole_content": true/false,
    "pattern": "decorative_correct" | "informative_correct" | "unlabeled_control" | "missing_label",
    "issues": [],
    "recommendation": "string or null"
  }
]

Data: [
  {
    "classes": "fas fa-search",
    "aria_hidden": true,
    "aria_label": null,
    "visible_text": null,
    "sibling_text": "",

api_result.response:
```json
[
  {
    "classes": "fas fa-search",
    "aria_hidden": true,
    "aria_label": null,
    "sibling_text": "",
    "sole_content": true,
    "pattern": "unlabeled_control",
    "issues": [
      "Icon is the sole content of its control (link or button) but aria-hidden=true hides it from screen readers",
      "Control has no accessible name — completely invisible to assistive technology"
    ],
    "recommendation": "Remove aria-hidden=true and add aria-label to the parent control (e.g., <button aria-label=\"Search\">) or add visually hidden text inside the control. The icon itself can then remain aria-hidden=true as a decorative supplement to that label."
  },
  {
    "classes": "fab fa-facebook",
    "aria_hidden": true,
    "aria_label": null,
    "sibling_text": "Facebook",
    "sole_content": false,
    "pattern": "decorative_correct",
    "issues": [],
    "recommendation": null
  },
  {
    "classes": "fab fa-x-twitter",
    "aria_hidden": true,
    "aria_label": null,
    "sibling_text": "X-twitter",
    "sole_content": false,
    "pattern": "decorative_correct",
    "issues": [],
    "recommendation": null
  },
  {
    "classes": "fab fa-youtube",
    "aria_hidden": true,
    "aria_label": null,
    "sibling_text": "Youtube",
    "sole_content": false,
    "pattern": "decorative_correct",
    "issues": [],
    "recommendation": null
  },
  {
    "classes": "fas fa-caret-down",
    "aria_hidden": false,
    "aria_label": null,
    "sibling_text": "",
    "sole_content": false,
    "pattern": "missing_label",
    "issues": [
      "aria-hidden is not set, so the screen reader will attempt to announce this icon",
      "No aria-label and no visible or sibling text — screen reader will read the raw class name or nothing meaningful",
      "Icon font pseudo-content (e.g., Unicode glyph) may be announced verbatim, creating a confusing experience"
    ],
    "recommendation": "Determine the icon's role in context. If it is purely decorative (e.g., a visual affordance alongside a labelled dropdown), add aria-hidden=\"true\". If it conveys state or meaning on its own (e.g., expand/collapse indicator), either add aria-label to the icon element or, preferably, add aria-expanded and an accessible name to the parent control and then set aria-hidden=\"true\" on the icon."
  },
  {
    "classes": "eicon-chevron-left",
    "aria_hidden": true,
    "aria_label": null,
    "sibling_text": "",
    "sole_content": false,
    "pattern": "decorative_correct",
    "issues": [],
    "recommendation": "Verify in context that the parent control (e.g., a carousel Previous button) carries its own accessible name. If the icon is the sole content of that control, this becomes an unlabeled_control issue — see fas fa-search above for the fix pattern."
  },
  {
    "classes": "eicon-chevron-right",
    "aria_hidden": true,
    "aria_label": null,
    "sibling_text": "",
    "sole_content": false,
    "pattern": "decorative_correct",
    "issues": [],
    "recommendation": "Same caveat as eicon-chevron-left: confirm the parent control has an accessible name independent of this icon."
  },
  {
    "classes": "far fa-building",
    "aria_hidden": true,
    "aria_label": null,
    "sibling_text": "",
    "sole_content": false,
    "pattern": "decorative_correct",
    "issues": [],
    "recommendation": "Confirm the surrounding context provides sufficient textual information so the icon is truly supplementary and not the sole conveyor of meaning (e.g., an address block heading)."
  },
  {
    "classes": "fas fa-envelope",
    "aria_hidden": true,
    "aria_label": null,
    "sibling_text": "",
    "sole_content": false,
    "pattern": "decorative_correct",
    "issues": [],
    "recommendation": "Confirm adjacent visible text (e.g., an email address or 'Email us' label) is present so the icon is purely decorative in context."
  },
  {
    "classes": "fas fa-phone-alt",
    "aria_hidden": true,
    "aria_label": null,
    "sibling_text": "",
    "sole_content": false,
    "pattern": "decorative_correct",
    "issues": [],
    "recommendation": "Confirm a visible phone number or label accompanies this icon so it remains decorative in context."
  },
  {
    "classes": "fas fa-home",
    "aria_hidden": true,
    "aria_label": null,
    "sibling_text": "",
    "sole_content": false,
    "pattern": "decorative_correct",
    "issues": [],
    "recommendation": "Confirm the parent link or surrounding element has an accessible name (e.g., 'Home') so the icon is supplementary. If this icon is the sole content of a Home link, reclassify as unlabeled_control and add aria-label=\"Home\" to the anchor."
  },
  {
    "classes": "fab fa-facebook-square",
    "aria_hidden": true,
    "aria_label": null,
    "sibling_text": "",
    "sole_content": false,
    "pattern": "decorative_correct",
    "issues": [],
    "recommendation": "Confirm the parent control (likely a social-media link) has an accessible name such as aria-label=\"Facebook\" on the anchor element. Without sibling text and with sole_content potentially being true at the link level, this could be an unlabeled_control — audit the rendered HTML to verify."
  }
]
```
